Plot a fitted decision-focused lasso

Source: R/plot.R
plot.DecisionFocusedLasso.Rd

Three views of a DecisionFocusedLasso fit, each a ggplot. The default "roles" view shows which features look weak at predicting cost yet move the decision, so the method kept them. "penalty" shows how much the decision focus eased the penalty on those features; "path" shows the coefficient path of the decision-focused fit.

Usage

# S3 method for class 'DecisionFocusedLasso'
autoplot(object, type = c("roles", "penalty", "path"), ...)

# S4 method for class 'DecisionFocusedLasso,missing'
plot(x, type = c("roles", "penalty", "path"), ...)

Arguments

object, x

A DecisionFocusedLasso object.

type

Which view to draw: "roles" (default), "penalty", or "path".

...

Unused, present for method compatibility.

Value

A ggplot object.

Details

Feature labels use ggrepel when it is installed, and ordinary text labels otherwise.

See also

dfl_fit(), regret() and its plot() for the held-out spread.

Examples

sim <- simulate_capital_allocation(60, 6, 6, seed = 1)
fit <- dfl_fit(
  sim$x, sim$cost, sim$scenario,
  problem = capital_allocation_problem(max_weight = 0.5),
  element_id = sim$element_id,
  control = dfl_control(seed = 1, n_splits = 5L)
)
plot(fit)

plot(fit, type = "penalty")
